subtasks.description: Query Salesforce licensing database to extract total licensed seats by license type and org, cross-referenced with actual seat assignments and last login timestamps to identify unutilized seats (licenses with no recent activity or unassigned seats)

Reconcile licensed seat count against actual assignments, calculate utilization rates, classify licenses as fully utilized, partially utilized, or unutilized, and prioritize recommendations for license reallocation or termination

plan.cost_rationale: This analysis requires querying a relational database of Salesforce license records (sql_gen, mid tier due to multi-table joins between license assignments, user activity logs, and org metadata) and then reconciling the results to classify utilization status and generate business recommendations (cross_recon, mid tier for multi-signal comparison and classification). The cross_recon includes the derivation of utilization metrics and renewal posture recommendations as part of its reconciliation logic, avoiding unnecessary extra subtasks. Both tasks are moderately complex but do not require frontier-level reasoning or multi-CTE SQL with window functions.

output.sql: SELECT cf.license_id, cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.user_id, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_licensed_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cf.event_type = 'seat_assignment' THEN cf.user_id END) AS assigned_seats, MAX(alf.access_time) AS last_login, CASE WHEN MAX(alf.access_time) IS NULL THEN 'Never Logged In' WHEN MAX(alf.access_time) < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days' THEN 'Inactive (90+ days)' WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cf.event_type = 'seat_assignment' THEN cf.user_id END) = 0 THEN 'Unassigned' ELSE 'Active' END AS utilization_status, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_cost_usd, CASE WHEN SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) > 0 THEN ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cf.event_type = 'seat_assignment' THEN cf.user_id END)::NUMERIC / SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) * 100, 2) ELSE 0 END AS utilization_pct FROM consumption_facts cf LEFT JOIN access_log_facts alf ON cf.user_id = alf.user_id AND alf.result = 'success' WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%salesforce%' GROUP BY cf.license_id, cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.user_id ORDER BY utilization_status DESC, total_cost_usd DESC
